characters.description: An exceptionally capable human with alien contacts who heads toward the Oslo emergency as transformed wildlife and abnormal mana spread. He is trying to protect his family while investigating a threat that ordinary authorities do not yet understand.
characters.name: Silas Renner

characters.description: Silas's sister, whose emerging fire mana shows that exposure is changing her. Her condition makes the growing catastrophe personal for Silas and drives him to prepare her for dangers he cannot yet explain fully.
characters.name: Silas's sister

characters.description: A medical professional who joins Silas, Dutch, and Henrik in Bergen for the dangerous journey to Oslo. She is determined to find her father despite the spreading monsters, failed infrastructure, and military perimeter around the disaster zone.
characters.name: Asta

characters.description: An older civilian who joins the journey from Bergen to Oslo to find his college-age daughter.
characters.name: Dutch

characters.description: A mechanic who joins the journey from Bergen to Oslo to reach his wife and children in the northern part of the city.
characters.name: Henrik

characters.description: Asta's father, whom Silas previously encountered in Oslo Prison. His hidden laboratory stood at the center of the Oslo event, linking him to Asta's obscured past and the spreading crisis.
characters.name: Emil Larsson

characters.description: Dutch's daughter, found alive with other students behind a barricaded dormitory door. Her rescue becomes more dangerous when the building completes its transformation into an uncommon dungeon around the survivors.
characters.name: Katie

characters.description: Silas's sister, endangered by a severe mana infection that can be stopped only if she undergoes induction before time runs out.
characters.name: Cecilia Renner

characters.description: Silas's mother, present at the Renner home while emergency responders treat Cecilia's dangerous mana condition.
characters.name: Silas's mother

characters.description: A ten-foot mana-infected squirrel boss whose aura initially makes Silas perceive it as harmless. It is the apparent source of the speaking threat in the altered forest near his home.
characters.name: Squirrel boss

characters.description: Silas's bonded Eidolon, restored in a stronger winged form with four arms, shields, and a spiked tail. He arrives during Silas's losing battle with the squirrel boss and immediately joins the fight as a coordinated companion.
characters.name: Erg

characters.description: Silas's mentor and a leading Rayden strategist on Galen. When Silas returns to blockaded Anwich, Samvek lays out the choice between resisting Transhek's control and negotiating with the corporations competing for access to Galen and Earth.
characters.name: Samvek

characters.description: A Galen companion who reunites with Silas inside blockaded Anwich.
characters.name: Dori

characters.description: A frontline Galen companion who reunites with Silas inside blockaded Anwich.
characters.name: Crag

characters.description: A Galen mage companion who reunites with Silas inside blockaded Anwich.
characters.name: Nevin

characters.description: Silas's Delmin companion, who returns with him to blockaded Anwich.
characters.name: Dejin

characters.description: An Earth Forerunner whom Silas suspects of steering Earth's competition toward an outside beneficiary. The suspicion remains unproven.
characters.name: Anika

characters.description: The Adventurers Guild's most powerful figure on Galen and a declared supporter of Silas there.
characters.name: Selmac

characters.description: A Rayden healer called to examine Silas after his feral evolution drives him to absorb mana directly from Samvek's weapon. She finds severe strain around his core but no condition recognized by the system.
characters.name: Talia

characters.description: A corporate representative who meets Silas in a dungeon to negotiate access to Earth. Bymorte projects supernatural trust and presses for an exclusive arrangement, forcing Silas to bargain while resisting both the offer and the aura behind it.
characters.name: Bymorte

characters.description: A rare-class Rayden clansman who pilots the cloaked shuttle used to penetrate Transhek's blockade. He carries the assault force to a lightly shielded carrier so the boarding teams can strike its mana drive.
characters.name: Captain Rogers

characters.description: A Rayden rogue assigned to the carrier assault. His communication ability links the two boarding teams after they split inside the ship, making him the operational connection between forces moving along separate routes.
characters.name: Simon

characters.description: An epic Transhek Senior Acquisitions Manager waiting inside the carrier's mana-drive room. He reveals that the raid was anticipated and recorded, then confronts the Rayden strike force with power beyond its ordinary defenders.
characters.name: Thelmin Araskin

characters.description: Transhek's head of security and a rare-core geomancer who accepts a Guild-regulated duel over control of Anwich. He combines sword work and anti-shield equipment with earth magic under rules that forbid intentional killing.
characters.name: Bek

characters.description: A Transhek leader who confronts the Adventurers Guild alongside Bek during the dispute over Anwich.
characters.name: Resha

characters.description: A local police officer inducted during the mana-infected squirrel emergency at the Renner home. She forms a core balanced between rogue and mage shards and swears to defend Silas's family while handling induction knowledge cautiously.
characters.name: Sue Smith

characters.description: A local police officer whom Silas guides through induction during the attack near his home. He builds a warrior-heavy core with rogue support and agrees to help protect the Renner family while keeping dangerous knowledge controlled.
characters.name: Officer Johnson

characters.description: A powerful Earth Forerunner whom Silas regards as a possible ally despite having no agreement with him yet. His high rank makes him one of the few humans potentially able to share the burden of Earth's approaching contest.
characters.name: Jiang Bai

characters.description: A high-ranked Earth Forerunner whom Silas treats as an active adversary. He suspects that she may be steering Earth's competition toward an outside interest, but her exact loyalties and actions remain unproven.
characters.name: Anika Patel

characters.description: One of Earth's active, high-ranked Forerunners. She stands alongside Emil Larsson, Anika Patel, and Jiang Bai in the ratings, but Silas has not yet established whether her plans will align with his efforts to organize Earth's defense.
characters.name: Nuri Khalid

characters.description: A Crembori crew member who identifies Earth's major mana concentrations and agrees to undergo induction. After Silas sponsors him, he becomes the proposed diplomatic representative for contact between the Crembori refugees and the United States.
characters.name: Dan Orr

characters.description: A senior U.S. military officer present among the national-security officials assembled to hear Silas at the White House.
characters.name: General Krulak

characters.description: A combat veteran who remains at the White House during Silas's arrival and chooses direct talks instead of further attempts to detain or attack him.
characters.name: The President

characters.description: A CIA field operative who joins the U.S. delegation sent to witness conditions around Oslo. Silas arranges a controlled kill against a restrained mana-infected creature, allowing her to undergo induction with the other observers.
characters.name: Sydney Bristow

characters.description: An allied alien military captain introduced to Earth's inducted forces shortly before the Wild Hunt. His soldiers join the developing training network, adding experienced off-world fighters to the people preparing Earth's defenses.
characters.name: Captain Reitz
